action.reasoning: Observation: data_check.py (167s) verified edge indices for 10k graphs — no invalid graphs. So crashes not due to data corruption. Past training attempts all segfaulted even with 1000 graphs and manual batching, yet test_env on 128 graphs succeeded. This suggests a memory corruption or cumulative GPU memory leak that appears after multiple batches. Decision: Write a thorough debug_loader.py that runs 500 graphs for 2 epochs, logs memory after each batch, and uses try/except to capture segfault location. If it succeeds, we'll know the pipeline works for larger sets; if it crashes, we'll see where. After debug, we will retrain on 1000–2000 graphs for a few epochs and submit eval to finally get a baseline metric and measure iteration time.
